data.title: $7,150 Price Target Emerges for Gold as Metal Hits New High
data.description:
Gold (GLD) prices crossed above the $4,800 level on Wednesday as investors looked for safety amid fresh tariff threats and fears of a global trade war. After a historic run in 2025, gold has entered 2026 with strong momentum, supported by geopolitical tensions, lower real interest rates, and a growing move away from the U.S. dollar. As a result, some forecasts are becoming especially aggressive, with ICBC Standard Bank strategist Julia Du saying that gold could climb as high as $7,150 if these trends remain in place.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Moreover, overall market expectations are turning increasingly bullish. In fact, analysts surveyed by the London Bullion Market Association (LBMA) see gold rising above $5,000 this year, driven by Federal Reserve easing expectations, falling U.S. real rates, and ongoing central bank buying. Unsurprisingly, the LBMA described gold as the standout asset following its record-breaking performance last year, which helps reinforce the view that current conditions remain highly supportive.

In addition, Goldman Sachs (GS) has called gold its highest-conviction trade, driven by a shift in demand from central banks to private investors, such as wealth managers, hedge funds, and pension funds. Importantly, analysts argue that this rally does not look like a speculative bubble but rather part of a longer-term trend that has been marked by ongoing geopolitical tensions and countries' increasing focus on securing critical resources.

Is Gold a Good Buy?

Using TipRanks' technical analysis tool, the indicators seem to point to a positive outlook for gold. Indeed, the summary section pictured below shows that 14 indicators are Bullish, compared to two Neutral and six Bearish indicators.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Netflix Stock (NFLX) Falls as Warner Deal Adds Debt — Is It Risky?
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) stock is down 4% as of this writing on Wednesday. The company has reportedly lined up an additional $8.2 billion in debt, strengthening its all-cash offer for Warner Bros. Discovery's (WBD) streaming and studio assets. For investors, it's reasonable to worry about higher leverage as Netflix is pulled into an expensive bidding war, hurting NFLX stock in the near term. However, the longer-term picture looks more encouraging.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

NFLX stock also fell due to soft Q1 guidance and lingering uncertainty around the Warner deal. Netflix reported its Q4 FY25 results, beating earnings estimates and showing solid subscriber growth. Meanwhile, Netflix switched its Warner offer to an all-cash deal, aiming to block rival Paramount Skydance (PSKY) in the bidding war.

Netflix Secures $8.2B in Debt

Banks, including Wells Fargo & Co., BNP Paribas, and HSBC, have begun selling $8.2 billion in short-term loans to help Netflix fund its planned acquisition. According to reports, the loans are being syndicated to the same banks that provided Netflix's earlier long-term financing. This additional bridge funding allows Netflix to make its bid entirely in cash, instead of the previous mix of cash and stock.

Netflix originally arranged $59 billion in bridge loans to back its bid. It later refinanced around $25 billion with longer-term loans, including two term loans and a revolving credit facility. With the latest syndicated loans, the company now has roughly $42.2 billion in bridge loans to refinance.

Should Investors Worry?

Investors remain concerned that the Warner Bros. deal could push Netflix into areas outside its core business, risking management losing focus. Netflix also said in Tuesday's shareholder letter that it will temporarily pause share buybacks to save cash for the deal, which may have added to investor worries.

Over the long term, if the Warner deal goes through, it could stabilize NFLX stock. Expanding content scale, increasing ad revenue potential, and adding valuable IP like HBO and Warner franchises could boost future cash flow and earnings growth.

Analysts also note that Netflix's fundamentals (subscriber base, ad growth) remain solid, which could support valuation once execution risks fade.

Is Netflix a Good Stock to Buy?

Currently, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Netflix stock based on 24 Buys, nine Holds, and two Sell recommendations. The average NFLX stock price target of $124.80 indicates 43.02% upside potential.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: 3 Best Dividend Aristocrat Stocks to Buy Now, 01/21/2026
data.description:
If building a dependable income portfolio is on your radar for 2026, dividend aristocrat stocks offer a compelling way to secure a consistent stream of cash payouts.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Which Dividend Aristocrat Stocks Are the Best to Buy?

According to Wall Street analysts, the three stocks listed below are worth buying. Each stock has a dividend payout ratio of at least 50%, meaning that each company distributes at least half of its net earnings to shareholders as dividends. Furthermore, each company currently has a Buy rating based on consensus from top analysts or broader Wall Street agreement, with significant upside potential.

Be sure to click on any ticker to thoroughly research the stock before you decide whether to add it to your portfolio. Here are today's top dividend aristocrat stock picks from TipRanks.

  1. Abbott Laboratories (ABT) — Abbott is an American healthcare company that manufactures drugs, diagnostic and nutritional products as well as medical devices. The Illinois-based company's payout ratio is currently 144.74%. Abbott paid 63 cents per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 1.97%. ABT stock currently boasts a Strong Buy consensus rating from 15 Wall Street analysts, with an average price target of $150.07, implying about 24% upside potential.
  2. Linde PLC (LIN) — Linde is an industrial gas and engineering giant that processes atmospheric gases such as oxygen, nitrogen, and argon. It also designs and constructs turnkey process plants for third-party customers. The UK-based company's dividend payout ratio currently stands at 74.75%. The firm paid $1.50 per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 1.37%. LIN stock currently enjoys a Strong Buy consensus from 17 analysts on Wall Street, with an average price target of $499.07 that suggests about 15% upside.
  3. Hormel Foods Corporation (HRL) — Hormel is an American food processing giant that delivers branded food products such as meat and nuts to retail as well as commercial customers. The Minnesota-based company currently has a dividend payout ratio of 169.71%. It paid 29 cents per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 4.80%. HRL stock currently holds a Moderate Buy consensus rating from five Wall Street analysts, with an average price target of $27.40, implying about 13% upside potential.

What Are Dividend Aristocrats?

Dividend aristocrats are S&P 500 (SPX) companies that not only deliver dividends every year, but have also raised those payouts annually for a minimum of 25 consecutive years. This kind of long-term, steady dividend growth makes them a popular choice for investors who care more about reliable income than about big, short-term swings in share prices.

Newly created spin‑off companies can also qualify as dividend aristocrats, because investors generally treat them as carrying over their former parent's long track record of annual dividend increases.

Interested in More Dividend Aristocrat Stocks?

To find more stocks like these, take a look at TipRanks' Dividend Aristocrats tool.

The page provides an exhaustive list of all dividend aristocrats, including their most recent dividend yields, previous dividend amounts, payout ratios, and other details.

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Gains as Trump Rules Out Using Force Against Greenland; Global Leaders Converge for WEF
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is in the green on Wednesday as investors digest comments from President Trump and other leaders at the World Economic Forum (WEF) Annual Meeting in Davos, Switzerland.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Trump ruled out using force to acquire Greenland and said that the military is off the table. "I don't have to use force. I don't want to use force. I won't use force. All the United States is asking for is a place called Greenland," he said. Afterward, he called for an immediate meeting to begin negotiations of the self-governing territory and emphasized its importance to national security.

Trump also said that he would ask Congress to enforce his proposed credit card interest rate limit of 10%, as it would help Americans save up money to purchase a home. The idea didn't sit well with JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon, who warned that it could lead to an "economic disaster."

"In the worst case, you'd have to have a drastic reduction of the credit card business," said Dimon at the WEF, adding that it could take away credit accessibility for 80% of Americans. Dimon jokingly proposed that Trump should first test the policy in Vermont and Massachusetts, the home states of Senators Bernie Sanders and Elizabeth Warren. Both Senators have previously expressed support for a credit card rate limit.

Meanwhile, Citadel CEO Ken Griffin said that Americans are responsible for paying the brunt of the Trump administration's tariffs. "So, the money raised by Washington in the tariffs has really come at the expense of the American people," he said at the WEF. A recent study released by the Kiel Institute echoed his claim, showing that Americans have absorbed 96% of the tariffs, while foreign exporters have shouldered just 4%.

The Dow Jones is up by 0.51% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: All Eyes on Microsoft Stock Ahead of Earnings Next Week; Here's What Wall Street Expects
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) shares have been on the back foot in recent months, and have retreated by 16% since the company reported fiscal first quarter (September quarter) earnings in late October. With the tech giant set to deliver its FQ2 results next Wednesday (Jan 28), investors will be hoping the report will bring about a shift in sentiment.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, despite noting that the company appears to be performing well, Cowen analyst Derrick Wood is skeptical that it is about to happen.

"While data points are strong, we think shares could remain range-bound until MSFT is able to deliver acceleration in Azure growth, which we don't anticipate for a couple more quarters," the analyst explained.

That's not to say Wood is taking a bearish stance. The analyst expects 37% (in constant currency) growth for Azure in Q2, in line with management's guidance of 37% and only slightly below the Street's 37.5%. Recall that Azure posted 39% constant-currency growth in Q1.

It's worth noting that Wood's checks and industry data indicate demand for compute across GPU and CPU workloads remains "highly encouraging," which he believes could ultimately add around 2 percentage points of upside to estimates, similar to the prior quarter. The analyst attributes the temporary slowdown in reaching 40%+ constant-currency growth mainly to the capacity constraints management highlighted in Q1. However, as additional capacity comes online in the second half of calendar 2026, Wood sees a path for Azure to re-accelerate into the 40%+ range, which could "help drive stronger stock performance & valuation uplift."

Wood also points to Cowen's 2026 Enterprise Software Survey featuring "bullish demand indicators." It ranks Microsoft first for AI spending intentions, with 76% of respondents planning to allocate new AI budgets to Microsoft over the next year, ahead of GCP at 66% and AWS at 59%. In terms of overall cloud spending plans, Microsoft also rose year-over-year to the top spot, surpassing AWS for the first time in the survey. AI budgets favoring Microsoft and the "positive spend intentions" for CPU workloads are both encouraging, as they are "strong directional trends for Azure."

As for the raw results, for FQ2, Wood is forecasting revenue of $80.7 billion, up 16% y/y (14% cc), slightly above the Street at $80.2 billion (up 15% USD). The analyst expects EPS of $3.98 compared to the Street at $3.93, a gross margin of 67.1% in line with consensus, and an operating margin of 45.4% vs. consensus at 45.6%.

Looking ahead to F3Q, Wood is calling for revenue of $81.7 billion, rising 17% (15% cc), above the Street's $81.2 billion forecast. Wood's EPS estimate lands at $4.05 vs. consensus at $3.98, with an operating margin of 45.5% matching the Street's forecast.

"Overall," Wood summed up, "we remain constructive on MSFT's medium-term positioning in AI & Cloud wallet capture."

As such, Wood assigns a Buy rating on MSFT shares, although given "recent pressure in the sector," his price target goes from $655 to $625. Nevertheless, the new target still makes room for 12-month returns of 41%. (To watch Wood's track record, click here)

Most analysts agree with that stance; MSFT stock claims a Strong Buy consensus rating, based on an uneven mix of 32 Buys and 2 Holds. The $630.64 average price target is almost the same as Wood's objective and suggests shares will climb 42.5% higher in the year ahead. (See MSFT stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Trump's Tariffs Have 'Come at the Expense of the American People,' Says Citadel's Ken Griffin
data.description:
Citadel CEO and founder Ken Griffin said on Wednesday that U.S. consumers have been paying the price for the Trump administration's tariffs.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"Unfortunately, the most recent research indicates the tax been borne by both American consumers and by American businesses," he said at the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting in Davos, Switzerland. "So, the money raised by Washington in the tariffs has really come at the expense of the American people."

Americans Pay for 96% of Tariffs, Shows Kiel Study

A study released by the Kiel Institute earlier this week found that Americans have absorbed 96% of the Trump administration's tariffs, while foreign exporters have incurred the remaining 4%. The German research firm analyzed shipment-level data across 25 million transactions worth nearly $4 trillion.

Kiel pointed to two event studies on Brazil and India, two countries that received relatively high tariff rates from the U.S. Export data showed that the tariffs didn't cause export prices to fall, and instead lowered trade volume. In addition, the study said that tariffs interfere with supply chains and buying habits while narrowing the selection of goods available for consumers.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: F5 Shareholders Sue Company Over Security Breach Claims
data.description:
A class action lawsuit was filed against F5 (FFIV) on December 19, 2025.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Plaintiffs in the federal securities class action allege that they acquired F5 stock at artificially inflated prices between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025, known as the "Class Period." They are now seeking compensation for financial losses incurred upon public revelation of F5's alleged misconduct during that time.

Investors who bought F5 stock during that period can click here to learn about joining the lawsuit.

F5 is a global multicloud application security and delivery company. It provides various distributed cloud services; application security and delivery products; and application management solutions. It also makes assorted professional services, such as maintenance, consulting, training, and extra technical support services available to its customers in both the public and private sectors.

Based in Seattle, F5 was founded in 1996 and now has approximately 6,500 employees. It used to be known as F5 Networks, Inc., and adopted its current moniker back in 2021.

Summary of Facts

The company and four of its senior officers (the "Individual Defendants") are now accused of deceiving investors by lying and withholding important information about F5's business and prospects during the Class Period.

In particular, they are accused of omitting truthful information about a security breach affecting the company from SEC filings and related material. By knowingly or recklessly doing so, they allegedly caused F5 stock to trade at artificially inflated prices during the time in question.

After a series of partial disclosures, the truth fully emerged after the market closed on October 27, 2025. That's when F5 announced its fourth quarter fiscal year 2025 results. In this context, the company disclosed "significantly below-market growth expectations for fiscal 2026," which it mostly blamed on  the Security Breach affecting F5. The company also announced "expected reductions to sales and renewals, elongated sales cycles, terminated projections, and increased expenses attributed to ongoing remediation efforts."
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Home Depot Stock (NYSE:HD) Gains Despite Growing Concerns About ICE Connection
data.description:
Home improvement giant Home Depot (HD) has been in the hot seat of late over what looks like connections to Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), and by extension, to the Trump administration. Investors are growing increasingly concerned about these connections, noted a new report from Reuters. But investors kept their skepticism limited, as evidenced by the nearly 1.5% gain in Home Depot stock seen in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The report noted that investors—backed up by a group with sustainability on its mind—are calling for a review of Home Depot's connection with Flock Safety. Flock Safety handles corporate security measures, including gathering license plate data. The report suggested that local law enforcement agencies were taking the license plate data Flock Safety generated, and passing that information on to ICE.

Most of this seems above board, so about the only place the investor coalition can object is at Flock Safety. The investor coalition wants an "…assessment of privacy and civil rights risks, including discrimination or wrongful detention from misuse of customer data." Such misuse may, in turn, "…expose the Company to financial and legal risks, including potential data breaches and enforcement of evolving state privacy laws."

Backing Up Disaster Recovery

Meanwhile, Home Depot Foundation is taking a step forward in trying to narrow the gap between skilled labor and disaster recovery, which is suffering from a shortage of exactly that. Thus, the Foundation is starting up new programs that "…focus on training and credentialing veterans and other workers for disaster recovery roles."

This notion became particularly compelling after the California wildfires, reports noted, when a clear call emerged for disaster recovery professionals to help rebuild communities. The Foundation's work with veterans, meanwhile, may help provide a ready and partially-trained labor pool for such efforts.

Is Home Depot a Good Long-Term Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on HD stock based on 17 Buys, five Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After an 8.53% loss in its share price over the past year, the average HD price target of $407.67 per share implies 7.11% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Up Today, 1/21/2026?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading higher on Wednesday after President Trump ruled out using force to acquire Greenland.

"I don't have to use force. I don't want to use force. I won't use force. All the United States is asking for is a place called Greenland," Trump said at the World Economic Forum Annual Meeting in Davos, Switzerland.

Trump Eyes Greenland Negotiations, Sees Ukraine-Russia Deal as Close

While the statement helped ease tensions between the U.S., Greenland, and the European Union, Trump added that he would seek immediate negotiations to begin the process of acquiring Greenland and highlighted the territory's importance to national security.

Meanwhile, Trump noted that a peace deal between Ukraine and Russia was reasonably close ahead of meetings with the presidents of both countries later this week. "If they don't get this done, they are stupid," he said.
